posts.author: Sergey1 (Serhii Idod)
posts.cookedHtml:
Can someone help me? I do have a google sheet with more than 50 columns which is exceeds monday.com import limit. I have

ve found a solution of it. In the same file I have created another sheet where create a query that pulling less than 50 columns from original sheet. When I`m trying to import the file, monday.com says that file too small. I assume that monday sees only the formula cell but not whole information that it pulls from another sheet.

sheetform790×47 4.98 KB

error790×482 20.2 KB

posts.author: CharlotteK (Charlotte Kirkham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey there! Could you try duplicating the Google Sheet and then deleting the second half of the columns in the first duplicate and the first half of the columns in the second duplicate? Then you could import the two duplicate Google Sheets to monday one at a time.

If you need further guidance, please reach out to our support team here.

posts.author: JoshFromUnito (Hugh Neato)
posts.cookedHtml: @Sergey1 How many rows/items are you working with? If you’re working with hundreds or thousands, your best bet is a 2-way sync solution like Unito. Here’s a guide on syncing Google Sheets rows to monday.com items and tasks.
